findings.title: Stored XSS on Home dashboard via unescaped trip name in the 'expenses recorded for {trip}' stat line
findings.rootCause: HomeHeader renders the stat line with `dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: t('home.tripStatsLine', { count: 'N', trip: activeTrip.name || '' }) }}`. The t() interpolator does a plain String(params[name]) substitution with NO HTML-escaping (i18n.ts:266-267), and the {trip} slot is filled with the raw user-controlled trip name. The template uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML only to bold the count, but the same unescaped path carries activeTrip.name into the DOM as live HTML. Server-side, upsert_trip binds t['name'] straight into the INSERT with only a presence check (trips.py:88) and no sanitization, and it round-trips raw via /api/data. Sibling code escapes the name correctly — trip-controls.ts:52 uses esc(t.name), TripBody.tsx:974/979 wrap with esc(...), Collections.tsx uses plain JSX {trip.name} — so this is an inconsistency that singled out the Home stat line.

findings.title: No map placeholder or error state when Google Maps fails to load — active-trip hero is a blank glass card
findings.rootCause: HeroMap's effect bails when typeof google === 'undefined' || !google.maps and calls whenGoogleMapsReady() (which rejects after a 10s timeout, googleMapsServices.ts:56-57). The rejection path only console.warns (HeroMap.tsx:162-164); nothing renders a placeholder. The #homeHeroMap div stays empty. The card is .cover-card cover-card--md, and .cover-card--md (home.css:483) sets only height + border/radius — no background-color — so the empty map area is just the parent .glass translucent blur. There is a country-select fallback for Maps-unavailable in the trip-create modal (task MK3-1) but the active-trip hero map has no equivalent.

findings.title: Personalized greeting silently always says 'traveler' — server never sends user.firstName
findings.rootCause: pickGreeting reads STATE.user.firstName (welcomeCard.ts:70) and falls back to 'traveler' when it's missing. But the server's user payload in EVERY auth endpoint (/api/me at auth.py:67-77, dev-login at 185-187, Google login at 262-264) only sends name, never firstName. The client assigns STATE.user = data.user verbatim (auth.ts:58), so user.firstName is always undefined. The rest of the codebase derives the first name from name (e.g. modals/trip.ts:160 STATE.user.name.split(' ')[0]), so welcomeCard.ts is the odd one out reading a field that's never populated.

findings.title: Fresh-trip greeting re-rolls (text changes) on any background state change because the random pick runs in render
findings.rootCause: HomeHeader calls pickGreeting(activeTrip, isFresh) directly in render (HomeHeader.tsx:64), and pickGreeting picks one of four templates with Math.random() each call (welcomeCard.ts:79). Home subscribes via useActiveTrip → useStore, which re-renders on every state:changed emit (store.ts bumps a version counter and useSyncExternalStore re-renders all subscribers). So any real STATE change re-runs the random pick and the H1 text changes. It only happens while isFresh is true (once a day/expense exists, pickGreeting returns the stable greetingDefault), which bounds the window — but it's a jarring, non-deterministic flicker during onboarding, the exact moment polish matters most.

findings.title: No-trip welcome slideshow shows a broken-image glyph (empty alt) when Unsplash is unreachable
findings.rootCause: The slideshow image element is emitted as `` (welcomeCard.ts:36) with no onerror fallback, and the 6s rotation just swaps imgEl.src (slideshow.ts:233) with no error handling. Every slideshow image is a hardcoded https://images.unsplash.com/... URL (constants.INSPIRATIONAL_PAIRS). When the fetch fails the browser shows its default broken-image placeholder; the empty alt means there isn't even descriptive text. (The active-trip hero is a map, not an img, so this is specific to the no-trip welcome path.)
